$comment: CortexBench V0 benchmark source manifest. Machine-readable source of truth for the Phase 4 loader. Verify license_spdx and redistribution_ok before fetching any dataset. See specs/license_audit.md for full detail.

sources.name: LIMIT
sources.description: On the Theoretical Limitations of Embedding-Based Retrieval. Proves embedding-based retrieval is bounded by dimension. 50k docs, 1k queries, 2k qrels.
sources.attribution: Weller et al., arXiv:2508.21038. © Google DeepMind. Licensed under Apache-2.0 (code) and CC-BY-4.0 (data).
sources.subset: full (limit) or sample (limit-small) — Phase 4 decides based on eval budget
sources.notes: Dual license: Apache-2.0 for code, CC-BY-4.0 for dataset and other materials. Repository footnote: 'not an official Google product.'

sources.name: MTEB
sources.description: Massive Text Embedding Benchmark — evaluation framework and task orchestration for retrieval tasks. Not a standalone dataset.
sources.attribution: MTEB authors. Apache-2.0. Per-task dataset attribution required separately.
sources.subset: Retrieval tasks — specific BEIR subsets selected in sources.json BEIR entry
sources.notes: MTEB is the evaluation framework; data license varies per task. Version v2.12.38 (2026-05-03).

sources.name: BEIR
sources.description: Heterogeneous Benchmark for Zero-shot IR. 15+ diverse retrieval datasets. Used as near-collision supplementary family alongside LIMIT.
sources.attribution: Thakur et al. BEIR paper + per-subset original paper citation required.
sources.subset: Recommended V0 subsets: NQ (Apache-2.0), HotpotQA (CC-BY-SA-4.0). Avoid MSMARCO and TREC-COVID until commercial-use review completed.
sources.notes: BEIR README: 'It remains the user's responsibility to determine whether you have permission to use the dataset under the dataset's license.' Phase 4 must verify each subset before loading. NQ and HotpotQA are safe defaults.

sources.name: LoCoMo
sources.description: Evaluating Very Long-Term Conversational Memory of LLM Agents. Synthetic dialogues up to 300 turns with stale-vs-current ground-truth labels.
sources.attribution: Maharana et al., arXiv:2402.17753. ACL 2024. CC-BY-NC-4.0.
sources.subset: Temporal subset: stale-vs-current labels for temporal update/revocation family
sources.notes: CC-BY-NC-4.0 NonCommercial clause is a BLOCKER for Phase 4 data loading in a commercial context. Required action: (A) obtain commercial license from Snap Research, OR (B) replace with a permissive alternative, OR (C) derive synthetic equivalent under Apache-2.0. Contact: adymaharana@cs.unc.edu. See specs/license_audit.md §4.

sources.name: MemoryAgentBench
sources.description: Evaluating Memory in LLM Agents via Incremental Multi-Turn Interactions. Includes EventQA and FactConsolidation tasks with incremental truth updates.
sources.attribution: Hu, Wang, McAuley. arXiv:2507.05257. ICLR 2026. MIT License.
sources.subset: Temporal tasks: EventQA, FactConsolidation. Also draws from RULER, InfBench, HELMET, LongmemEval (upstream licenses to be verified per-subset by Phase 4).
sources.notes: MIT license confirmed via HuggingFace dataset card only. Phase 4 should verify a LICENSE file exists in the GitHub repo. Upstream datasets (RULER, InfBench, HELMET, LongmemEval) have their own licenses — Phase 4 must audit if loading those subsets directly.

sources.name: MemoryArena
sources.description: Benchmarking Agent Memory in Interdependent Multi-Session Agentic Tasks. Human-crafted multi-session agentic tasks across shopping, planning, search, and formal reasoning domains.
sources.attribution: He et al., arXiv:2602.16313. February 2026. Dataset: CC-BY-4.0 via HuggingFace ZexueHe/memoryarena.
sources.subset: All five configurations: bundled_shopping, progressive_search, group_travel_planner, formal_reasoning_math, formal_reasoning_phys
sources.notes: Dataset CC-BY-4.0 confirmed via HuggingFace. Code repository URL unresolved as of 2026-05-05 — project website links to github.com homepage only. Contact zexueh@stanford.edu to confirm code repo. Pin commit hash once confirmed. Phase 4 can load from HF dataset without the code repo.

sources.name: WARP
sources.description: WARP: An Efficient Engine for Multi-Vector Retrieval. XTR/ColBERT-based engine. Motivates multi-vector slot design in CortexState. Not a data source.
sources.attribution: Scheerer et al., arXiv:2501.17788, SIGIR 2025. MIT License.
sources.notes: WARP is design motivation, not a data source. CortexBench does not load WARP task data. XTR upstream: google-deepmind/xtr, Apache-2.0 / CC-BY-4.0, commit 52d5b5ec796f51a8eb76aa727873545a58ce8b80 (2024-06-20).
